reasoning: A formal two-week ceasefire regarding direct soil-to-soil strikes was agreed upon on April 8, 2026. While a potentially violating strike hit Iran's Lavan Island roughly 8 hours into the ceasefire, Iran's retaliation was directed at the UAE, Kuwait, and Saudi Arabia rather than US or Israeli soil, preserving the technical conditions of the 14-day pause against qualifying military actions. With the 14-day clock likely starting on April 8 or 9, it would conclude around April 22-23 — leaving a buffer before the April 30 deadline. The Polymarket price is at 72.5% as of April 11, indicating confidence but acknowledging the fragility of the truce (due to ongoing strikes in Lebanon and threats regarding the Strait of Hormuz). We align with this market consensus but adjust slightly upward over hypothetical longer periods given the creative circumvention strategies (hitting third parties instead of Israel/US) being employed to maintain the technical ceasefire.
